bibliographic_data.title: Replacing gas boilers with heat pumps is the fastest way to cut German gas consumption
bibliographic_data.abstract: The supply security of fossil gas has been disrupted by the Russo-Ukrainian War. Decisions to relocate the production and transport of gas have become so urgent that new long-term contracts are imminent, undermining the Paris Climate Agreement. Here, we simulate how quickly the addition of renewable electricity and installation of heat pumps can substitute enough gas to reduce supply risk while taking a decisive step towards meeting the Paris Agreement.

core_content.novelty_claim: The paper proposes a pathway for reducing German gas consumption by installing heat pumps, which is one of the fastest ways to achieve this goal.
core_content.key_methodology: Bottom-up modeling using Germany as an example, focusing on hourly resolution of gas and electricity consumption in buildings, industry, and power generation.
outcomes: Technical pathways showing how heat pumps can substitute up to 60% of Russian gas by 2025
Demonstration that grid expansions will allow sufficient renewable electricity for both heating and other sectors
core_content.full_text_markdown: The paper addresses the urgent need to reduce German gas consumption, particularly from Russia, due to supply disruptions caused by the Russo-Ukrainian War. The authors propose a pathway involving the rapid installation of heat pumps powered by renewable electricity as one of the fastest methods to achieve this goal. They use bottom-up modeling with Germany as an example, focusing on hourly resolution for gas and electricity consumption in buildings, industry, and power generation. The study shows that up to 60% of Russian gas can be substituted by 2025 through heat pumps and grid expansions, while still allowing for the phase-out of coal and entry into e-mobility.
